text.title: Growth Community Building Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Growth Community Building application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Launch on exactly one platform; multi-platform launches are rejected.
rule.rationale: Multi-platform splits seed energy and creates duplicate-conversation entropy.

rule.statement: ≥5 conversation prompts MUST be planted before the first invitation wave; empty-room launches are rejected.
rule.rationale: Members joining an empty room churn within 7 days; planted conversations seed the social proof loop.

rule.statement: A single named human MUST own the rituals; team-owned communities default to no-one.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; rituals need a face to deliver them.

rule.statement: ≥3 rituals MUST run on a published cadence (daily/weekly/monthly); ad-hoc engagement is rejected.

rationale.source: The Business of Belonging — David Spinks

rule.rationale: Rituals are the heartbeat; without them attention attrites and members lurk.

rule.statement: Moderation MUST be light-touch with published guidelines; pre-approval queues are rejected.
rule.rationale: Heavy moderation kills spontaneity; members self-censor when they feel monitored.
